_readme: Config-as-DATA for the #25 Evolution skills lane (added 2026-07-11). The daily landscape monitor (scripts/landscape-monitor.js) reads this to scan agent-skill directories; the weekly installer (scripts/skills-installer.js) reads the trust_allowlist + weekly_install_cap + audit rules. Editing this file changes behavior WITHOUT touching code (SOURCES-as-data ground rule). API shapes below were probed live 2026-07-11; if a directory changes its JSON, fix the `map` here, not the monitor. skillhub.club is DISCOVERY-ONLY: its github_stars are whole-repo/mirror-bot stars (noise), its own simple_score is null, and installs always resolve to + audit the GitHub source (never its one-click). The trust_allowlist is the real filter, not popularity.
_cap_note: The 'safe != free' budget guard (skills-sh-sweep.md): every installed skill taxes every session's context + matching surface. Never auto-install more than this many per weekly eval; the rest fall to the digest.
_max_log_note: Hard ceiling on skill rows the monitor appends per run, strongest candidates first (real install counts before repo stars). Keeps the weekly eval prompt bounded. Stars-based directories (skillsmp/skillhub) additionally log ONLY trust_allowlist authors, since their star counts are whole-repo/mirror-bot noise.
_keywords_note: Derived from the manifest project portfolio + their domains. Keep the list <= 45 so a single daily run stays under skillsmp.com's 50-req/day anon cap. The eval judges fit against the real projects; these just cast the net.
_allowlist_note: Author namespaces (the owner segment of owner/repo) eligible for AUTO-INSTALL. A skill from any other author is still discovered + logged + can appear in the digest, but is NEVER silently installed (it routes to 'Flagged, manual review'). This is what neutralizes skillhub.club mirror bots (clawdbot, openclaw, etc.). Seeded with the already-installed sources (skills-lock.json) + official orgs (makenotion, openai). Shaheen edits to add trust; the installer also auto-trusts any author already present in skills-lock.json.

directories.name: skillsmp.com
directories._note: 50 req/day anon cap. Gives repo stars (weak signal), not installs; used mainly to widen discovery + confirm skills.sh finds. Repo resolved by parsing owner/repo out of github_url.

directories.name: skillhub.club
directories._note: Low trust (your 07-11 finding). github_stars is whole-repo/mirror-bot noise, simple_score is null. Discovery only: never installed except via allowlist + GitHub-source audit. repo_url like https://github.com/owner/repo#... -> owner/repo.

audit._note: Deterministic source-level audit run by skills-installer.js BEFORE any npx skills add. Any hit here = the skill is NOT installed and drops to the digest as 'Flagged, manual review'. This is the automated validation gate that stands in for the removed human gate.
audit._tokens_note: Genuine execution/destruction signals only. Substrings like 'token'/'secret'/'process.env' were removed - they false-positive on knowledge skills that merely DISCUSS tokens/env, and the allowed_outbound_domains check below is what actually catches data exfiltration.
